LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DEBORAH CULLEN'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY IS GRANTED. MS. CULLEN HAS MET HER BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT VENUE IS NOT PROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY. NEITHER MS. CULLEN NOR MS. AYUSO RESIDE IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY. NOR DOES THIS COUNTY HAVE JURISDICTION OVER THE TRUSTS FOR WHICH MS. CULLEN AND MS. AUUSO ARE TRUSTEES. THERE ARE NO STATUTORY EXCEPTIONS CONFERRING PROPER VENUE IN A COUNTY OTHER THAN THE COUNTIES IN WHICH DEFENDANTS RESIDE OR THE COUNTIES WHICH HAVE JURISDICTION OVER THE TRUSTS. "INJURY TO PERSON OR PROPERTY" AS THAT PHRASE IS USED IN CCP 395(A) IS LIMITED TO PHYSICAL OR CORPOREAL INJURY AND DOES NOT INCLUDE FINANCIAL INJURIES. WELFARE & INSTITUTIONS CODE 15657.3(A) IS INAPPLICABLE BECAUSE IT ADDRESSES JURISDICTION RATHER THAN VENUE. PLAINTIFF HELEN GUGHEMETTI SHALL PAY ALL TRANSFER COSTS. MS. CULLEN'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. MS. GUGHEMETTI'S CHOICE OF VENUE, WHILE INCORRECT, WAS NOT MADE IN BAD FAITH AND HAD AN ARGUABLE BASIS IN LAW AND FACT. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976; DEPT. 302.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-13-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-14-2016 Continued To Feb-01-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Joinder In Part And Opposition In Part To Defendant Deborah Cullen'S Motion To Transfer Case
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Mary Catherine Ayuso's joinder in part and opposition in part to Ms. Cullen's motion to transfer venue is denied as to the request to transfer this case to Merced County. Because Ms. Cullen is the moving defendant, the case is transferred to Los Angeles County, the venue Ms. Cullen selected. Counsel for Defendant is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Action; Request For $2,610.00 In Monetary Sanctions Against Attorney Sean Patrick Miller, Esq.
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Deborah Cullen's motion to transfer venue to Los Angeles County is granted. Ms. Cullen has met her burden of showing that venue is not proper in San Francisco County. Neither Ms. Cullen nor Ms. Ayuso reside in San Francisco County. Nor does this county have jurisdiction over the trusts for which Ms. Cullen and Ms. Auuso are trustees. There are no statutory exceptions conferring proper venue in a county other than the counties in which defendants reside or the counties which have jurisdiction over the trusts. "Injury to person or property" as that phrase is used in CCP 395(a) is limited to physical or corporeal injury and does not include financial injuries. Welfare & Institutions Code 15657.3(a) is inapplicable because it addresses jurisdiction rather than venue. Plaintiff Helen Gughemetti shall pay all transfer costs. Ms. Cullen's request for sanctions is denied. Ms. Gughemetti's choice of venue, while incorrect, was not made in bad faith and had an arguable basis in law and fact. Counsel for Defendant is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
